page: Amazon Web Services g4dn.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 2 threads per core running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance includes a dedicated NVIDIA T4 GPU from the Turing architecture family with 16 GB of VRAM, alongside 125 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. While general-purpose CPU and memory operations score in the lower percentiles, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU tests consistently falling into the weak or poor categories, the instance excels in GPU-driven workloads. Specifically, LLM inference benchmarks for small 135M and medium 7B models achieve top-tier and strong ratings, demonstrating high efficiency in prompt processing and text generation.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g4dn.xlarge offers a specialized entry point for GPU-accelerated tasks without the overhead of larger multi-GPU systems. The tradeoff involves modest CPU performance and memory bandwidth in exchange for dedicated hardware acceleration. This makes the instance highly suitable for small-to-medium machine learning inference, graphics-intensive applications, and lightweight CUDA-based processing, rather than compute-heavy CPU tasks or high-throughput database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. The hardware configuration includes a dedicated NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM and 125 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While benchmarks indicate weak performance for general CPU, memory, and database operations, the instance delivers top-tier results in LLM inference for small and medium models. This creates a clear tradeoff, making the g4dn.xlarge highly cost-efficient for specialized GPU tasks like model inference and graphics processing, while being less suited for general-purpose compute or high-throughput CPU workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an Intel Xeon CPU, 16 GB RAM, a dedicated NVIDIA T4 GPU, and 125 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for small-to-medium LLM inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with an Intel Xeon CPU, 16 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4 GPU for machine learning workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring a dedicated NVIDIA T4 GPU and NVMe storage, optimized for small-to-medium language model inference workloads.

Dedicated NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB VRAM for hardware acceleration
125 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
